name: TitleMax Title Loans
description_short: TitleMax offers secured title pledges and unsecured flex lines of credit with same-day cash approval, accepting most credit types at 1,000+ locations nationwide.
description_long:
TitleMax is one of the largest title lending companies in the United States, with this Memphis Summer Avenue location open since February 2007. The company operates a broad retail footprint across the South and Midwest, serving borrowers who need fast access to cash and may not qualify for traditional bank products. TitleMax is owned by TMX Finance LLC, a privately held consumer lending group headquartered in Savannah, Georgia.

This location offers two primary product lines. The first is a secured title pledge — commonly called a title loan — where borrowers use their clear vehicle title as collateral to access a lump sum of cash. The lender considers the vehicle's value, the amount requested, and the borrower's ability to repay. Borrowers keep and continue driving their vehicle while the loan is active. The second product is an unsecured flexible line of credit, available both in-store and online, which functions like a revolving credit line that can be drawn down all at once or over time up to a set limit. Online unsecured loans are restricted to residents of AZ, DE, ID, KS, MO, MS, SC, TN, TX, UT, and WI.

What distinguishes TitleMax at this location includes bilingual staff, extended weekday hours (9 AM–7 PM Monday and Friday), and a 5.0/5 Google rating based on over 481 reviews — with reviewers repeatedly citing specific staff members by name for professionalism and helpfulness. The Memphis Summer Avenue location also has several nearby sister stores, making in-person support accessible across the city.

Honestly, TitleMax fills a real gap for borrowers with imperfect credit and paid-off vehicles who need same-day funds. However, title loans are among the most expensive consumer credit products available — APRs can reach triple digits — and defaulting puts the borrower's vehicle at risk of repossession. The website does not disclose interest rates or APR ranges, which makes comparison shopping difficult. Borrowers should exhaust payday alternative loans (PALs), employer advances, and credit union emergency loan options before using a title loan.
pros: Same-day cash disbursement at in-store locations
Accepts most credit types — vehicle title can substitute for strong credit score
Borrowers keep and drive their vehicle throughout the loan term
Bilingual staff available at the Memphis Summer Avenue location
Offers unsecured line of credit in addition to secured title products
5.0/5 Google rating from 481+ verified customer reviews at this location
Memphis Summer Avenue store has been operating continuously since February 2007
cons: Requires a clear (lien-free) vehicle title — borrowers who still owe on their car do not qualify
Website does not disclose APR ranges, making cost comparison impossible before applying
Title loans carry repossession risk if the borrower defaults
Online unsecured line of credit restricted to only 11 states
Closed Sundays at this location, limiting same-day access over weekends
best_for: Borrowers with a paid-off vehicle who need fast cash and have poor or thin credit
Tennessee residents needing same-day emergency funds without a traditional bank option
Borrowers who want a revolving line of credit rather than a fixed lump-sum loan
Spanish-speaking borrowers needing bilingual lending assistance
diagnosis: TitleMax is best suited for borrowers with a fully paid-off vehicle, urgent cash needs, and credit too weak for conventional lenders. The main caveat is cost: title loans are high-APR products and the website discloses no rate information, so borrowers should request the full APR in writing before signing and treat this as a last resort after exhausting lower-cost emergency options.
services: Unsecured personal line of credit (in-store and online)
Partial or lump-sum drawdowns on line of credit
